en.extract:
The Sunda crow (Corvus enca) is a passerine bird of the family Corvidae that is found in South-East Asia, from Malaysia to Borneo. The Sunda crow is part of the Corvus enca species complex, which was formerly treated as a single species and known as the slender-billed crow. The complex is now treated as five species, after four subspecies were split off as distinct species: the Samar crow or small crow (Corvus samarensis), the Palawan crow (Corvus pusillus), the Sulawesi crow (Corvus celebensis) and the Sierra Madre crow (Corvus sierramadrensis). The violet crow was also once included, but has been shown to be distinct genetically and separated as Corvus violaceus.


Taxonomy
Corvus enca was formally described in 1821 by the American naturalist Thomas Horsfield based on a specimen collected on the Indonesian island of Java. He coined the binomial name Fregilus enca. The specific epithet enca is a Javanese word for a crow.
Corvus enca is part of a species complex, which was previously treated as a single species with six subspecies, then known of as the slender-billed crow. Four of those subspecies are now treated as a separate species:

Sulawesi crow (Corvus celebensis)
Samar crow (Corvus samarensis)
Sierra Madre crow (Corvus sierramadrensis)
Palawan crow (Corvus pusillus)
The remaining two subspecies are recognised as subspecies of the Sunda crow:

C. e. compilator Richmond, 1903 – Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, west Sumatran islands and Borneo
C. e. enca (Horsfield, 1821) – Java and Bali
In earlier treatments, the Banggai Crow (Corvus unicolor) and the Violet Crow (Corvus violaceus) were also included in Corvus enca.


Habitat and diet
It is found throughout Maritime Southeast Asia (Brunei, Indonesia and the Philippines) and Peninsular Malaysia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical mangrove forest. It eats fish and shrimp.

The '''Sunda crow''' ('''''Corvus enca''''') is a [[passerine]] bird of the family [[Corvidae]] that is found in South-East Asia, from Malaysia to Borneo. The Sunda crow is part of the ''Corvus enca'' species complex, which was formerly treated as a single species and known as the '''slender-billed crow'''. The complex is now treated as five species, after four subspecies were split off as distinct species: the [[Samar crow]] or small crow (''Corvus samarensis''), the [[Palawan crow]] (''Corvus pusillus''), the [[Sulawesi crow]] (''Corvus celebensis'') and the [[Sierra Madre crow]] (''Corvus sierramadrensis'').<ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-05-29|language=en-US}}</ref> The [[violet crow]] was also once included, but has been shown to be distinct genetically and separated as ''Corvus violaceus''.<ref name="jonsson 2012">{{cite journal|author1=Jønsson, Knud A. |author2=Fabre, Pierre-Henri |author3=Irestedt, Martin |year=2012|title=Brains, tools, innovation and biogeography in crows and ravens|journal=BMC Evolutionary Biology|volume=12|url= |doi=10.1186/1471-2148-12-72 |page=72 |pmid=22642364 |pmc=3480872 |doi-access=free |bibcode=2012BMCEE..12...72J }}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
''Corvus enca'' was [[species description|formally described]] in 1821 by the American naturalist [[Thomas Horsfield]] based on a specimen collected on the Indonesian island of [[Java]]. He coined the [[binomial name]] ''Fregilus enca''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Horsfield | first=Thomas | author-link=Thomas Horsfield | year=1821 | title=Systematic arrangement and description of birds from the island of Java | journal=Transactions of the Linnean Society | volume=13 | issue=1 | pages=133–200 [164] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/754897 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1962 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=15 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=264 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14485637 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''enca'' is a [[Javanese language|Javanese]] word for a crow.<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=enca | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=enca | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=7 September 2024 }}</ref>

''Corvus enca'' is part of a species complex, which was previously treated as a single species with six subspecies, then known of as the slender-billed crow. Four of those subspecies are now treated as a separate species:<ref name=ioc/>
* [[Sulawesi crow]] (''Corvus celebensis'')
* [[Samar crow]] (''Corvus samarensis'')
* [[Sierra Madre crow]] (''Corvus sierramadrensis'')
* [[Palawan crow]] (''Corvus pusillus'')

The remaining two [[subspecies]] are recognised as subspecies of the Sunda crow:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Crows, mudnesters, birds-of-paradise | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/crows/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=15 September 2024 }}</ref>
* ''C. e. compilator'' [[Charles Wallace Richmond|Richmond]], 1903 – [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Sumatra]], west [[Sumatran]] islands and [[Borneo]]
* ''C. e. enca'' ([[Thomas Horsfield|Horsfield]], 1821) – [[Java]] and [[Bali]]

In earlier treatments, the [[Banggai Crow]] (''Corvus unicolor'') and the [[Violet Crow]] (''Corvus violaceus'') were also included in ''Corvus enca''.<ref>{{cite BOW|title=Slender-billed Crow |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/slbcro1/1.1/introduction#sys|access-date=8 July 2025}}</ref>

==Habitat and diet==
It is found throughout [[Maritime Southeast Asia]] ([[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]] and the [[Philippines]]) and [[Peninsular Malaysia]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical [[mangrove forest]]. It eats fish and shrimp.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Eddy | first1=S. | last2=Mutiara | first2=D. | last3=Mediswati | first3=R.Y.T. | last4=Rahman | first4=R.G. | last5=Milantara | first5=N. | last6=Basyuni | first6=M. | date=2021 | title=Short communication: Diversity of bird species in Air Telang Protected Forest, South Sumatra, Indonesia | journal=Biodiversitas Journal of Biological Diversity | volume=22 | issue=12 | doi=10.13057/biodiv/d221206 | doi-access=free }}</ref>

zh.extract:
细嘴乌鸦（学名：Corvus enca），是鸦科鸦属的一种，分布于文莱、印度尼西亚、马来西亚和菲律宾。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
细嘴乌鸦的平均体重约为251.0克。栖息地包括城市、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带严重退化的前森林、亚热带或热带的红树林和耕地。
